title: Multiplex cellular reference materials
abstract: Disclosed are nucleic acids comprising a plurality of nucleotide sequences, wherein each nucleotide sequence corresponds to a genotype. The nucleic acids are useful for developing biological reference materials comprising a number of different genotypes.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1. A nucleic acid, comprising a plurality of nucleotide sequences, wherein: each nucleotide sequence of the plurality comprises a first subsequence and a second subsequence; the first subsequence comprises a 3' sequence of a first exon; the second subsequence comprises a 5' sequence of a second exon; the first subsequence and second subsequence are adjoining sequences in the nucleic acid; the first subsequence is 5' relative to the second subsequence in the nucleic acid; the first exon is an exon of a first gene; the second exon is an exon of a second gene; the first gene and second gene are different genes; and each nucleotide sequence of the plurality of nucleotide sequences comprises either a 3' sequence of a first exon that is different from every other first exon of the nucleotide sequences of the plurality or a 5' sequence of a second exon that is different from every other second exon of the nucleotide sequences of the plurality. 2. The nucleic acid of claim 1, wherein the nucleic acid is DNA. 3. The nucleic acid of claim 1, wherein the nucleic acid is RNA. 4. The nucleic acid of any one of claims 1-3, wherein each nucleotide sequence of the plurality is associated with a neoplasm. 5. The nucleic acid of claim 4, wherein the neoplasm is a lung cancer, non-small cell lung cancer, soft tissue cancer, lymphoid cancer, acute lymphoid leukemia, acute myeloid leukemia, chronic myelogenous leukemia, non-Hodgkin's lymphoma, Burkitt lymphoma, melanoma, intraocular melanoma, central nervous system cancer, neuroblastoma, thyroid cancer, parathyroid cancer, hepatocellular cancer, stomach cancer, large intestine cancer, colon cancer, urinary tract cancer, bladder cancer, kidney cancer, prostate cancer, cervical cancer, ovarian cancer, or breast cancer. 6. The nucleic acid of any one of claims 1-5, wherein each first gene and each second gene is selected from the group consisting of anaplastic lymphoma receptor tyrosine kinase (ALK), brain-specific angiogenesis inhibitor 1 -associated protein 2-like protein 1 (BAIAP2L1), CD 74, echinoderm microtubule-associated protein-like 4 (EML4), ETS variant 6 (ETV6), fibroblast growth factor receptor 3 (FGFR3), kinesin-1 heavy chain (KIF5B), nuclear receptor coactivator 4 (NCOA4), nucleophosmin (NPMl), neurotrophic tyrosine receptor kinase 1 (NTRK1), neurotrophic tyrosine receptor kinase 3 (NTRK3), paired box gene 8 (Pax8), peroxisome proliferator-activated receptor gamma (PPARG), RET proto-oncogene (RET), ROS proto- oncogene 1 (ROS1), sodium-dependent phosphate transport protein SLC34A, transforming acidic coiled-coil-containing protein 3 (TACC3), TRK-fused gene (TFG), and tropomyosin 3 (TPM3). 7. The nucleic acid of claim 6, wherein each first exon and each second exon is selected from the group consisting of ALK exon 20, BAIAP2L1 exon 2, CD74 exon 6, EML4 exon 13, ETV6 exon 5, FGFR3 exon 18, KIF5B exon 24, NCOA4 exon 8, NPMl exon 5, NTRKl exon 10, NTRK3 exon 13, PAX8 exon 8, PPARG exon 1, RET exon 11, RET exon 12, ROS1 exon 34, SLC34A exon 4, TACC3 exon 11, TFG exon 5, and TPM3 exon 8. 8. The nucleic acid of claim 7, wherein the first exon and second exon of each nucleotide sequence is selected from the group consisting of EML4 exon 13 and ALK exon 20; NPMl exon 5 and ALK exon 20; KIF5B exon 24 and Ret exon 11; NCOA4 exon 8 and RET exon 12; CD74 exon 6 and ROS1 exon 34; SLC34A exon 4 and ROS1 exon 34; TPM3 exon 8 and NTRKl exon 10; TFG exon 5 and NTRKl exon 10; FGFR3 exon 18 and BAIAP2L1 exon 2; FGFR3 exon 18 and TACC3 exon 11; PAX8 exon 8 and PPARG exon 1; and ETV6 exon 5 and NTRK3 exon 13, respectively. 9. The nucleic acid of any one of claims 1-5, wherein each nucleotide sequence of the plurality comprises a subsequence of a gene selected from the group consisting of anaplastic lymphoma receptor tyrosine kinase (ALK), brain-specific angiogenesis inhibitor 1 -associated protein 2-like protein 1 (BAIAP2L1), CD74, echinoderm microtubule-associated protein-like 4 

 (EML4), ETS variant 6 (ETV6), fibroblast growth factor receptor 3 (FGFR3), kinesin-1 heavy chain (KIF5B), nuclear receptor coactivator 4 (NCOA4), nucleophosmin (NPM1), neurotrophic tyrosine receptor kinase 1 (NTRK1), neurotrophic tyrosine receptor kinase 3 (NTRK3), paired box gene 8 (Pax8), peroxisome proliferator-activated receptor gamma (PPARG), RET proto- oncogene (RET), ROS proto-oncogene 1 (ROSl), sodium-dependent phosphate transport protein SLC34A, transforming acidic coiled-coil-containing protein 3 (TACC3), TRK-fused gene (TFG), and tropomyosin 3 (TPM3). 10. The nucleic acid of claim 9, wherein each subsequence of a gene is a subsequence from a single exon of the gene. 11. The nucleic acid of claim 10, wherein each single exon is selected from the group consisting of ALK exon 20, BAIAP2L1 exon 2, CD74 exon 6, EML4 exon 13, ETV6 exon 5, FGFR3 exon 18, KIF5B exon 24, NCOA4 exon 8, NPM1 exon 5, NTRKl exon 10, NTRK3 exon 13, PAX8 exon 8, PPARG exon 1, RET exon 11, RET exon 12, ROSl exon 34, SLC34A exon 4, TACC3 exon 11, TFG exon 5, and TPM3 exon 8. 12. The nucleic acid of claim 11, wherein the first exon and second exon of each nucleotide sequence are selected from the group consisting of EML4 exon 13 and ALK exon 20; NPM1 exon 5 and ALK exon 20; KIF5B exon 24 and Ret exon 11 ; NCOA4 exon 8 and RET exon 12; CD74 exon 6 and ROSl exon 34; SLC34A exon 4 and ROSl exon 34; TPM3 exon 8 and NTRKl exon 10; TFG exon 5 and NTRKl exon 10; FGFR3 exon 18 and BAIAP2L1 exon 2; FGFR3 exon 18 and TACC3 exon 11 ; PAX8 exon 8 and PPARG exon 1 ; and ETV6 exon 5 and NTRK3 exon 13, respectively. 13. The nucleic acid of any one of claims 1-15, wherein the plurality of nucleotide sequences consists of 2 to 12 nucleotide sequences. 17. The nucleic acid of claim 15 or 16, wherein the plurality of nucleotide sequences consists of 6 nucleotide sequences. 18. The nucleic acid of any one of claims 1-17, wherein each first subsequence and each second subsequence is at least 50 nucleotides long. 19. The nucleic acid of claim 18, wherein each first subsequence and each second subsequence is 120 to 500 nucleotides long. 20. The nucleic acid of any one of claims 1-19, wherein: 

 a first subsequence or a second subsequence of a nucleotide sequence of the plurality comprises two or more exons; each exon of the two or more exons is an exon of the same gene; and the exons of the two or more exons are ordered in the nucleic acid according to the order of the exons in a naturally-occurring mRNA. 21. The nucleic acid of claim 20, wherein: a first subsequence comprises two or more exons, and the first exon of the first subsequence is less than 200 nucleotides long; or a second subsequence comprises two or more exons, and the second exon of the second subsequence is less than 200 nucleotides long. 22. The nucleic acid of claim 20 or 21, wherein the sum of the lengths of the two or more exons is at least 50 nucleotides long. 23. The nucleic acid of claim 22, wherein the sum of the lengths of the two or more exons is 120 to 500 nucleotides long. 24. The nucleic acid of any one of the preceding claims, further comprising a nucleotide sequence comprising an intron, wherein: the nucleotide sequence comprising an intron comprises a first subsequence and a second subsequence; the first subsequence comprises a 3 ' subsequence of an intron or exon of a first gene; the second subsequence comprises a 5' subsequence of an intron or exon of a second gene; the first subsequence and second subsequence are adjoining sequences in the nucleic acid; the first subsequence is 5' relative to the second subsequence; and the first gene and second gene are the same gene or different genes. 25. The nucleic acid of claim 24, wherein: 

 the first subsequence comprises a 3 ' subsequence of an intron of a first gene; or the second subsequence comprises a 5' subsequence of an intron of a second gene. 26. The nucleic acid of claim 24 or 25, wherein the first gene and second gene of the nucleotide sequence comprising an intron are different genes. 27. The nucleic acid of claim 24 or 25, wherein the first gene and second gene of the nucleotide sequence comprising an intron are the same gene. 28. The nucleic acid of any one of claims 1-27, further comprising a poly-A tail. 29. The nucleic acid of any one of claims 1-28, further comprising at least one methylated nucleoside. 30. The nucleic acid of claim 29, wherein the at least one methylated nucleoside comprises 7- methyl guanosine. 31. The nucleic acid of claim 30, wherein the nucleic acid comprises a 5' [m7G(5')ppp(5')G] cap. 32. The nucleic acid of any one of claims 1-31, further comprising a promoter. 33. The nucleic acid of claim 32, wherein the promoter is from a different species than the nucleotide sequences of the plurality. 34. The nucleic acid of claim 32 or 33, wherein the promoter is an SP6 promoter. 35. The nucleic acid of any one of claims 1-34, wherein the nucleic acid is a plasmid. 36. A method for making the nucleic acid of any one of claims 1-31, comprising incubating a reaction mixture comprising a DNA template, RNA polymerase, and ribonucleotide 

 triphosphates at a temperature at which the RNA polymerase displays polymerase activity, thereby making the nucleic acid. 37. A composition comprising a plurality of nucleic acid fragments, wherein: each nucleic acid fragment of the plurality of nucleic acid fragments is a fragment of a nucleic acid according to any one of claims 1-34; and each nucleotide sequence of the plurality of nucleotide sequences of the nucleic acid is encoded by at least one nucleic acid fragment of the plurality of nucleic acid fragments. 38. The composition of claim 37, wherein the sequence assembly of the nucleotide sequences of the nucleic acid fragments of the plurality results in nucleotide sequences that align with at least 50% of the nucleotide sequence of the nucleic acid. 39. The composition of claim 38, wherein the sequence assembly of the nucleotide sequences of the nucleic acid fragments of the plurality results in a nucleotide sequence that aligns with 100% of the nucleotide sequence of the nucleic acid. 40. A composition comprising a plurality of nucleic acid fragments, wherein the sequence assembly of the nucleotide sequences of the nucleic acid fragments of the plurality results in a nucleotide sequence that aligns with 100% of the nucleotide sequence of a nucleic acid according to any one of claims 1-34. 41. The composition of claim 40, wherein each nucleotide sequence of the plurality of nucleotide sequences of the nucleic acid is encoded by at least one nucleic acid fragment of the plurality of nucleic acid fragments. 42. The composition of claim 40 or 41, wherein each nucleic acid fragments of the plurality of nucleic acid fragments is a fragment of the nucleic acid. 43. The composition of any one of claims 37-42, wherein the plurality of nucleic acid fragments consists of fragments or degradation products of the nucleic acid. 44. The composition of any one of claims 37-43, wherein the nucleotide sequence of the nucleic acid may be identified by sequencing the plurality of nucleic acid fragments by next generation sequencing. 45. The composition of any one of claims 37-44, further comprising substantially all of the transcriptome of a cell. 46. The composition of claim 45, wherein the ratio of the copy number of each nucleotide sequence of the plurality of nucleotide sequences to a single copy of the transcriptome of the cell is about 1 : 10 to about 1000: 1 in the composition. 47. The composition of claim 46, wherein the ratio of the copy number of each nucleotide sequence of the plurality of nucleotide sequences to a single copy of the transcriptome of the cell is about 1 : 1 to about 100: 1 in the composition. 48. The composition of any one of claims 45-47, wherein the composition further comprises the cell. 49. The composition of any one of claims 45-48, wherein the cell is a human cell. 50. The composition of any one of claims 45-49, wherein the cell is a fibroblast or a lymphocyte. 51. The composition of claim 50, wherein the cell is an immortalized B lymphocyte. 52. The composition of claim 51, wherein the cell is GM24385. 53. The composition of any one of claims 48-52, wherein the composition comprises a plurality of cells, the cell is a cell of the plurality of cells, and each cell of the plurality of cells comprises substantially the same genome. 54. The composition of claim 53, wherein each nucleic acid fragment of the plurality of nucleic acid fragments is an intracellular nucleic acid. 55. The composition of claim 54, wherein each nucleic acid fragment of the plurality of nucleic acid fragments is a nucleic acid fragment that has been transfected into a cell or a fragment thereof. 56. The composition of any one of claims 53-55, wherein each cell of the plurality of cells is fixed. 57. The composition of claim 56, wherein each cell of the plurality of cells has been fixed with formalin. 58. The composition of any one of claims 53-57, wherein the cells of the composition are embedded in paraffin. 59. The composition of any one of claims 37-47, wherein the composition does not comprise a cell. 60. The composition of claim 59, wherein the plurality of nucleic acid fragments has been extracted from a cell. 61. The composition of any one of claims 37-60, wherein the composition comprises urea, guanidine, an RNAse inhibitor, ethylenediaminetetraacetate, a protease, a DNAse, ethanol, isopropanol, or a reverse transcriptase. 62. The composition of any one of claims 37-61, wherein each nucleic acid fragment of the plurality of nucleic acid fragments is associated with a solid support. 63. The composition of claim 62, wherein the solid support comprises magnetic beads. 64. A cell comprising the nucleic acid of any one of claims 1-35. 65. The cell of claim 64, wherein the cell is a human cell. 66. The cell of claim 64 or 65, wherein the cell is a fibroblast or a lymphocyte. 67. The cell of claim 66, wherein the cell is an immortalized B lymphocyte. 68. The cell of any one of claims 65-67, wherein the cell is GM12878, GM24149, GM24143, GM24385, GM24631, GM24694, or GM24695. 69. The cell of claim 68, wherein the cell is GM24385. 70. The cell of any one of claims, 64-69 wherein the cell comprises 1 to 1000 copies of the nucleic acid. 71. The cell of claim 70, wherein the cell comprises 5 to 500 copies of the nucleic acid. 72. The cell of claim 64, wherein the cell is E. coli. 73. A composition, comprising a first plurality of cells and a second plurality of cells, wherein: the first plurality of cells consists of cells according to claim 71 ; the second plurality of cells consists of cells that do not comprise the nucleic acid; the first plurality of cells and the second plurality of cells are human cells; the first plurality of cells and the second plurality of cells are admixed in the composition; and the ratio of the number of cells of the first plurality to the number of cells of the second plurality is about 1 : 1 to about 1 : 10,000 in the composition. 74. A method for making a biological reference material, comprising transfecting a plurality of cells with the nucleic acid of any one of claims 1-35 or the plurality of nucleic acid fragments of any one of claims 37-44. 75. The method of claim 74, further comprising fixing the cells of the plurality. 76. The method of claim 75, wherein fixing the cells comprises fixing the cells with formalin. 77. The method of any one of claims 74-76, further comprising embedding the cells in paraffin. 78. The method of any one of claims 74-77, further comprising diluting the plurality of cells with untransfected cells. 79. The method of claim 78, wherein diluting the plurality of cells with untransfected cells comprises diluting at a ratio of transfected cells to untransfected cells of about 1 : 1 to about 1 : 10,000. 80. A biological reference material, comprising a plurality of cells of any one of claims 67-74 and paraffin, wherein the plurality of cells are fixed and embedded in the paraffin. 81. The reference material of claim 80, further comprising untransfected cells, wherein the untransfected cells do not comprise the nucleic acid or the plurality of nucleic acid fragments. 82. The reference material of claim 81, wherein the ratio of cells comprising the nucleic acid or the plurality of nucleic acid fragments to untransfected cells is about 1 : 1 to about 1 : 10,000. 83. The reference material of claim 82, wherein the ratio of cells comprising the nucleic acid or the plurality of nucleic acid fragments to untransfected cells is about 1 :5 to about 1 : 5,000. 84. The reference material of claim 83, wherein the ratio of cells comprising the nucleic acid or the plurality of nucleic acid fragments to untransfected cells is about 1 : 10 to about 1 : 1,000. 85. A biological reference material, comprising a plurality of cells of any one of claims 64- 71 ; and a liquid. 86. The reference material of claim 85, wherein the liquid is blood plasma. 87. A composition comprising a nucleic acid and an aqueous buffer, wherein the nucleic acid is a nucleic acid that has been extracted from the reference material of any one of claims 80-86. 88. The composition of claim 87, wherein the buffer comprises tris(hydroxymethyl)aminomethane and ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid, or a salt of any one of the foregoing.
